title: Abort: `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` in `_PyType_LookupStackRefAndVersion` (`typeobject.c:6343`)
description: `Objects/frameobject.c`: `PyFrame_GetBack()` lazily allocates the parent `PyFrameObject`; under OOM that fails and sets `MemoryError`, but the failure is not propagated -- `frame.f_back` returns `None` with the exception still pending, so the next `LOAD_ATTR` trips `_PyType_LookupStackRefAndVersion`'s `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` and aborts (release builds: swallowed error later surfaces as a spurious `SystemError`).
notes: Root cause is build-agnostic and lives in PyFrame_GetBack (Objects/frameobject.c:2395): _PyFrame_GetFrameObject -> _PyFrame_MakeAndSetFrameObject allocates the parent PyFrameObject and returns NULL+MemoryError under OOM, but PyFrame_GetBack returns NULL at L2407 without propagating, and frame_back_get_impl (L1116) converts NULL to None at L1118, swallowing the pending exception. The LOAD_ATTR opcode reports success with MemoryError still set, so the next LOAD_ATTR's _PyType_LookupStackRefAndVersion trips assert(!PyErr_Occurred()) at typeobject.c:6343. Symptom by build: abort on ft_debug_asan AND jit (both have assertions); on ft_release/upstream (-DNDEBUG) the assert is compiled out and there is NO crash -- the dangling MemoryError instead surfaces later as 'SystemError: <built-in function ...> returned a result with an exception set' (recorded n/a per assert-abort convention; note it does NOT segfault for the minimal repro, unlike some siblings). Minimized to a stdlib-only frame-walk (f.f_back) + a trailing LOAD_ATTR; deterministic at start=1 on ft_debug_asan and jit. f_locals/f_globals raise MemoryError cleanly; only f_back swallows it. 16 vehicles share the exact typeobject.c:6343 assertion via diverse frame-walkers: gettext._as_int2, logging.findCaller, asyncio extract_stack/traceback.walk_stack, argparse, optparse, concurrent.futures. Fix: propagate the alloc failure from PyFrame_GetBack and only map a clean NULL (no pending error) to None in frame_back_get_impl. FOLDED IN (2026-06-25): OOM-0011 (the LOAD_ATTR specializer `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())`@specialize.c:364) was a second DETECTOR face of THIS f_back-swallow bug -- rr on OOM-0011's optparse.ngettext repro watched tstate->current_exception and reversed to the SAME producer (_PyErr_NoMemory <- _PyObject_GC_NewVar(PyFrame) <- PyFrame_GetBack <- frame_back_get_impl reading f_back). The earlier OOM-0011 'flakiness' (drift between specialize.c:364 and typeobject.c:6343) was always this one bug hitting two different `!PyErr_Occurred()` checkpoints. Added OOM-0011's detector sites (specialize:364 / specialize_module_load_attr_lock_held / _Py_Specialize_LoadAttr) to sites[] so LOAD_ATTR-specialize-assert crashes dedup here. DEDUP CAVEAT: stale-exception crashes are backtrace-undisambiguable -- the producer (f_back) already returned and is NOT on the crash stack, so the ONLY usable key is the detector assert site (typeobject.c:6343 / specialize.c:364). These are producer-AGNOSTIC: almost always this f_back-swallow (every vehicle is a frame-walker), but a stale exception from a different producer reaching these asserts would mislabel to OOM-0008 -- rr-confirm if a case looks off (same heuristic-with-audit stance as OOM-0023's msgfam).
